meta.title: OpenCairn — the AI knowledge OS that understands what you read
meta.description: Drop in your sources and 12 AI agents weave them into a wiki, surface connections before you ask, and measure your depth of understanding. Self-hosted, AGPLv3.

hero.label: [ 001 / 012 · HERO ]
hero.sub: Drop in PDFs, papers, videos, and podcasts. 12 AI agents weave them into a wiki, surface connections before you ask, and measure how well you actually understand what's there.
hero.noCard: No card required · all 12 agents included

spec.parsersVal: pdf · docx · hwp · epub · mp3 · mp4

activity.title: LIVE · WORKSPACE
activity.footer: Real agent activity, up to seconds ago

items.text: 4 new question candidates indexed · Q&A ready
Attention score heatmap · rendered in browser

rotating: Librarian: 3 duplicate merges suggested with existing wiki

pipeline.label: [ 003 / 012 · PIPELINE ]
pipeline.sub: Instead of one giant prompt, narrow-responsibility steps hand off the baton. That way the system knows when to stop and what to fix. Every sentence traces back to the source.

steps.title: Intake
steps.body: PDF · HWP · audio · video · URL · scanned images. Format-agnostic.

steps.title: Break down
steps.body: OCR · captions · table structure. Normalized to Markdown.

steps.title: Meaning
steps.body: Mapped to vector space. Similar things end up close.

steps.title: Weave
steps.body: 12 agents draft wiki pages per topic.

steps.title: Link
steps.body: Backlinks · opposing views · causal edges into a knowledge graph.

citation.label: ↪ CITATION
citation.title: Every sentence traces back to the source.
citation.body: AI-written sentences carry citations, and citations point to the materials you actually read. No hallucinations, no plagiarism — just what you already have.

agents.label: [ 004 / 012 · AGENTS ]
agents.sub: Instead of one giant prompt, narrow-responsibility agents hand off the baton. Each runs in a background that doesn't die — close the browser and your knowledge keeps growing.

items.name: Compiler
items.body: Extracts concepts from uploads into wiki pages, with two-way links built in.

items.name: Research
items.body: Hybrid Q&A across multiple search strategies. Every answer cited.

items.name: Librarian
items.body: Finds orphan pages, contradictions, and duplicates. Wiki health on a schedule.

items.name: Synthesis
items.body: Finds structural analogies across domains — immune system ↔ resilience.

items.name: Connector
items.body: Suggests cross-project links; accept to create them.

items.name: Temporal
items.body: Tracks how understanding evolves. Stale-knowledge warnings and review nudges.

items.name: Curator
items.body: Finds external sources to fill gaps in what you know.

items.name: Deep Research
items.body: Investigates one topic autonomously over a long horizon, then merges into the wiki.

items.name: Socratic
items.body: Checks understanding with flashcards and quizzes. Spaced repetition on weak spots.

items.name: Narrator
items.body: Turns wiki content into two-voice podcasts. Review while you walk.

items.name: Code
items.body: Generates Python/JS and runs it safely in the browser. Zero server code.

items.name: Visualization
items.body: Renders the graph as Graph · Mindmap · Cards · Canvas · Timeline.

views.label: [ 005 / 012 · VIEWS ]
views.title: One graph, five lenses.
views.sub: One dataset, five ways to see it. Switch to whichever view fits the moment. Every switch is logged and reversible.

items.name: Graph
items.body: Explore the full network at a glance

items.name: Mindmap
items.body: Radiate outward from a central concept

items.name: Cards
items.body: Search, filter, and spaced-repetition review

items.name: Canvas
items.body: Freeform layout for thinking in space

items.name: Timeline
items.body: How your understanding changes over time

try.label: [ 006 / 012 · TRY IT ]
try.sub: A small slice of a wiki built by a grad student who uploaded twenty transformer papers. Hover or tap a node — the connections OpenCairn found ripple out.
bullets: Each node is an auto-generated wiki page.
Edges are relationships found via embeddings and shared concepts.
The numbers show how often other pages reference this one.
try.caption: Knowledge graph · 8 nodes · 9 edges

attention.d: A mechanism that weighs different parts of the input sequence so the model can focus on what matters.

transformer.d: A sequence-to-sequence architecture built from attention alone. Encoder–decoder structure.

multihead.d: Runs attention in parallel subspaces to capture different kinds of relationships.

positional.d: Attention has no innate notion of order — position must be injected explicitly.

encoder.d: Stack that turns input tokens into representation vectors. The upper half of a Transformer.

decoder.d: Uses encoder output to generate the target sequence autoregressively.

bert.d: Encoder-only bidirectional pretrained model. Strong on understanding tasks.

rope.d: Position info via rotation matrices. Better extrapolation than naive positional encodings.

pretraining.d: Learn general language ability on a large corpus, then transfer to downstream tasks.

workspace.label: [ 007 / 012 · WORKSPACE ]
workspace.title1: Same shape for a solo user
workspace.sub: Wiki pages aren't just text — they're the meeting point of sources, agents, and teammates. Every page carries its citations, editors, and agent activity with it.

mock.body: Attention weights the tokens of an input sequence by relevance. Vaswani et al. (2017) — "Attention Is All You Need" — introduced it as the foundation of the transformer architecture; self-attention and multi-head attention followed.
mock.calloutBody: Three sources disagree on quadratic complexity. The Linear Attention family challenges this assumption.

feed.text: contradiction detected in source 4 vs source 9

principles.label: [ 008 / 012 · PRINCIPLES ]
principles.sub: Data sovereignty · living knowledge · one throughline to learning · open source. Tools that keep one or two of these are common. Keeping all four at once — that's OpenCairn.

items.title: Your data stays on your server.
items.body: Text, vectors, and original files all live in your infrastructure. In fully-local LLM mode there are zero outbound requests — deployable even where external SaaS is forbidden.

items.title: AI does the sorting, you do the judging.
items.body: The Compiler agent extracts concepts, creates pages, and links them to the existing wiki. You review and edit. The time you spent copying by hand disappears.

items.title: Capture → explore → learn, one continuous line.
items.body: Q&A · 5-view graph · spaced-repetition flashcards · Socratic dialogue · audio podcasts — all fed from the same wiki. No tool-hopping.

items.title: AGPLv3 in the open; exit always available.
items.body: Fork, modify, deploy yourself — whenever you want. Internal company use is unrestricted; commercial licenses exist if source disclosure is a problem. No risk of the service disappearing.

personas.label: [ 009 / 012 · WHO ]
personas.sub: Linearly scales from an individual to a lab to a regulated enterprise — without swapping tools.

items.title: Grad student · researcher
items.body: Fifty papers become one woven wiki. Explore relationships in the concept graph, delegate deep dives to Deep Research, and self-check before exams with AI quizzes. Citations lead straight back to your original notes.

items.title: Lab · small team
items.body: Run it with role-based permissions. Lab-meeting recordings auto-summarize into action items. Seniors graduate, but the knowledge stays behind — onboarding new members is fast.

items.title: Regulated industries
items.body: Designed for finance, healthcare, government, and universities — places where external SaaS isn't allowed. Fully-local LLM, enterprise SSO, audit logs, commercial licensing. Not a byte leaves your premises.

install.label: [ 010 / 012 · INSTALL ]
install.sub: One Docker Compose line is enough. Database, file storage, and workflow engine all come up together, and a single profile flag switches to fully-local LLM mode.
bullets: Minimum 4 vCPU · 8GB RAM, recommended 8 vCPU · 16GB RAM
x86_64 + ARM64 multi-arch (Raspberry Pi · Apple Silicon · Oracle Ampere)
Monthly automated backup-restore rehearsal script included
install.terminalTail: $ docker compose up → http://localhost:3000 → it just runs.

pricing.label: [ 011 / 012 · PRICING ]
pricing.sub: Try every agent on Free — no card required. When you scale into a lab or team, Pro removes the limits; bring your own Gemini key with BYOK.

free.tagline: Forever free, no card · for trying it out

secondary.label: ↪ OTHER ROUTES
secondary.title: Run it yourself, or on-prem.
secondary.sub: If managed cloud isn't for you, here are two more paths.

selfHost.title: Run it yourself with Docker
selfHost.body: Full open-source code (AGPLv3) · unlimited workspaces and members · fully-local LLM option. Hardware and ops are on you.

enterprise.title: On-prem · SSO · audit logs
enterprise.body: Dedicated install and support for finance, healthcare, government, and universities — environments where external SaaS isn't an option. Fully-local LLM · SAML/OIDC SSO · commercial license.

items.a: All of it in infrastructure you operate. Text, vectors, and wiki pages in the database; original files in file storage. In Gemini mode only the AI call leaves; in fully-local mode there are no outbound requests.

items.q: Doesn't AGPLv3 mean my company can't use it?
items.a: Internal company use is entirely fine. AGPLv3 triggers source-disclosure only when you serve third parties over a network. Sharing within a company counts as use, not distribution. A commercial license is available if source disclosure would be a problem.

items.q: Why not OpenAI instead of Gemini?
items.a: v0.1 supports Gemini (hosted & BYOK) and Ollama (fully local). We lean into Gemini-specific capabilities — large-context caching, native audio generation, real-time web search — as core value. For fully-local requirements, Ollama.

items.q: Can I migrate from my existing note app?
items.a: Export to Markdown, upload, and Compiler re-weaves it into a wiki. The pages you organized by hand come back as a connected wiki. Notion · Obsidian · Bear and other major export formats are supported.

items.q: What if the AI gets the wiki wrong?
items.a: AI-generated sentences are marked and always directly editable. Once you hand-edit a section, AI won't overwrite it. Librarian surfaces contradictions, every change lives in the activity log, and you can roll back to any point.

items.q: How smooth is the real-time collaboration?
items.a: We use Hocuspocus-based modern sync, so conflict resolution and "reconnecting…" delays are basically gone. Cursors and avatars, block-level comments, @mentions, notifications, public links, and guest invites are all in v0.1.

cta.label: ↪ CLOSING
cta.sub: Start with Free today — no card required. How much your knowledge base has grown a year from now depends on what you start accumulating today.
cta.bottomTonight: Tonight, start with your first PDF.

footer.tagline: AI knowledge OS. Solo or team. Self-hosted, forever.
footer.taglineMono: for people who read too much and remember too little.
